description:
Build the static HTML documentation site(s) from the FlowDoc markdown using the 
md-to-html skill. Converts __docs/ -> docs/maintainer/ and/or _docs/ -> 
docs/user/. Standalone capability (the old DocsToHTML / HTMLSiteBuild flow); 
pour it after a generate or maintain pass. The md-to-html skill is PREFERRED but 
OPTIONAL: the build step resolves it from several locations and, if it is not 
vendored in the target repo, falls back to an explicit portable build contract 
so the site still builds faithfully. Run via 'bd mol pour flowdoc-html 
--var target=both|maintainer|user'.

target.description: both = build maintainer (__docs/->docs/maintainer/) and user (_docs/->docs/user/); maintainer or user = build just one.

steps.title: FlowDoc: build HTML site ({{target}})
steps.description:
Umbrella for building the static HTML doc site(s) from FlowDoc markdown.
target = {{target}}. One authored child does the build.

steps.title: FlowDoc: convert markdown docs to the static HTML site ({{target}})
steps.description:
BUILD the HTML site(s) from FlowDoc markdown using the md-to-html skill.
target = {{target}}.

STEP 0 — RESOLVE THE SKILL (H1 portability). The md-to-html skill is the source
of truth for the build, but it is NOT guaranteed to be vendored in the target
repo. Resolve it in this order and record in your notes which one you used:
  a. <repo>/skills/md-to-html/SKILL.md (vendored in the target repo), else
  b. <repo>/.beads/skills/md-to-html/SKILL.md (beads-local copy), else
  c. the flowdoc toolkit checkout (e.g. ~/code/flowdoc/skills/md-to-html/), else
  d. NO skill found -> fall back to the EXPLICIT BUILD CONTRACT in STEP 3, which
     reproduces the skill's output shape with a generic md->html toolchain (a
     Markdown library plus the styling/JS described). NEVER silently skip the
     build because the skill is absent.
If a SKILL.md is found, read it for the exact build commands/options and prefer
it; the STEP 3 contract is the portable floor, not a replacement.

STEP 1 — maintainer site. If target is 'maintainer' or 'both':
  convert __docs/*.md -> docs/maintainer/*.html (mirror the directory tree).
STEP 2 — user site. If target is 'user' or 'both':
  convert _docs/*.md -> docs/user/*.html the same way.

STEP 3 — EXPLICIT BUILD CONTRACT (must hold whether or not the skill ran, H2).
Whatever toolchain you use, the published site MUST carry over, faithfully:
  - Mermaid diagrams: render fenced ```mermaid blocks (client-side mermaid.js
    initialized on load, or pre-rendered SVG). A raw code block is a FAIL.
  - GitHub-style callouts: > [!NOTE]/[!TIP]/[!WARNING]/[!IMPORTANT] blockquotes
    become styled callout boxes, not literal "[!NOTE]" text.
  - Per-section index pages: one index.html per doc directory linking its pages
    (mirror the markdown index/_Manifest navigation; do NOT publish the raw
    _Manifest).
  - style.css: a single shared stylesheet linked by every page (readable type,
    code/table styling, callout colors).
  - Relative-link rewrite: in-repo .md links are rewritten to the matching .html
    so navigation resolves inside the built site.

STEP 4 — landing page. Build/refresh the top-level docs/index.html linking the
maintainer and/or user site(s) that were built.

STEP 5 — VERIFY (gate):
  - every markdown source has a matching .html output (source<->output parity);
  - every internal link resolves in the built HTML (no dangling hrefs);
  - mermaid blocks and callouts render (no raw fences / literal "[!...]" text);
  - no _Manifest / _UpdateQueue / _AuditLog working files leak into docs/.

READ-ONLY source code. Output only under docs/. Do NOT close any bead yourself —
the LLM judges close it.
